content.problemStatement: Identify and correct the logic to print **true** if a number is even, else **false**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output "true" if N is even, otherwise output "false". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case with N = 5. 5 is odd, so the program prints false.
There is a single test case with N = 8. 8 is even, so the program prints true.
Three test cases: 2 is even → true, 7 is odd → false, 0 is even → true.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each integer N, check the remainder when divided by 2 (N % 2). If the remainder is 0 the number is even, otherwise it is odd. Print the corresponding string. The operation is O(1) per test case, so the total time is O(T). Only O(1) extra space is needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
